message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>At present, no unifying animal study to quantify the effect of PI on innate immunity in animals has been reported. This study quantifies the effect of PI in mice and THP-1 cells using a multi-disciplinary approach employing flow cytometry to measure immune function and mass spectroscopy to identify metabolomic profiles of treated cells. For proof of principle, a case study was conducted to examine the potential benefit of administering PI to improve outcomes of a feline leukemia-positive cat that also contracted the effusive form of FIP. Our results indicate that quantifiable cellular and metabolic markers result from PI treatment and can be used to establish a platform measuring the efficacy of PI in modulating the innate immune system.</jats:p>
title: Pre-Clinical Evaluation of a Novel Immunomodulator: a potential immunotherapy for coronaviral disease
